summary: Partial 9/20/68 Berkeley soundboard with clear stage banter and standout drum work, but the set is incomplete and musical performance is mixed.
review: The recording captures a decent‑quality soundboard tape from late September ’68 (likely Berkeley), with crisp stage chatter and especially strong drum moments that many fans enjoy. The show itself is only partially represented – missing “Schoolgirl” and other material – and the performances are judged average, with “St. Stephen” and a brief “Dark Star” getting modest praise while “Eleven” feels short‑changed.

ai_rating.rationale: Average show quality (70% weight) combined with generally good but incomplete recording (30% weight); reviewers average ~3.4 stars, so rating leans slightly higher due to positive comments on drums and clarity.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear stage banter and decent fidelity for a 1968 tape; some reviewers note the recording is "quite good" but the source is partial, with missing portions of the set.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The tape is incomplete, making the flow feel disjointed; a 25‑minute drum solo dominates the second half, which some listeners love while others find it overly long.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar work includes a strange vibrating tone behind “Several Seasons” and he laughs during his vocal on St. Stephen.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum work is highlighted as fantastic, with an extended 25‑minute solo that is a major draw for listeners.
